[624]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今春小住金阊 - Phrase meaning 'this spring, staying briefly in Jinchi,' where Jinchi refers to Suzhou (also known as Jinling).
  • 友人道及少卿校书，津津有味 - Means 'my friends spoke of Ms. Shaoqing, the librarian, with great interest,' where '津津有味' conveys enthusiasm or delight.
  • 欢生却扇 - A phrase from a classical allusion, meaning 'joyful interaction,' often associated with romantic encounters.
  • 韵度绕梁 - Means 'melodious and lingering,' often used to describe beautiful or lasting impressions.
  • 遂深悦其丰姿 - Means 'thus deeply admiring her graceful appearance.'
  • 倾倒其谈吐 - Means 'admiring her eloquent speech.'
  • 弦歌之下，又工吟咏 - Means 'under the music, she is also skilled in poetry.'
  • 近日吴下各姝，殆无其匹也 - Means 'among the beauties of Wu recently, none can match her.'
  • 仆以徘徊未久，惜又言旋 - Means 'I lingered not long enough, and then had to leave.'
  • 复读《申报》，知清和之多情，喜云英之早嫁 - Means 'I read the *Shen Bao* newspaper and learned that Qinghe is very affectionate and happy that Yunying has married early.'
  • 窃为少卿幸也 - Means 'I secretly feel fortunate for Ms. Shaoqing.'
  • 始知身虽跨凤，却又未能忘情于野鸳鸯 - Means 'I now know that although she has married into a noble family, she still has feelings for her former lover.'
  • 然与种情者，固应如是 - Means 'but for those who have cultivated feelings, it should be this way.'
  • 而于薄幸者，又当何如耶 - Means 'and what about those who are unfaithful?'
  • 仆之用情，虽较异于玉峰樵客 - Means 'My feelings, though different from those of the Woodcutter of Yufeng Mountain.'
  • 爰和原韵四章 - Means 'I have composed four poems in response to the original rhyme.'
  • 薄游犹记暮春天 - Means 'A brief visit still recalls the evening of spring.'
  • 拥髻联吟夜未眠 - Means 'Holding hairpins, we composed poetry together through the night.'
  • 谁料销魂真个后，行云何处认良缘 - Means 'Who would have thought that after such a passionate encounter, where can the floating clouds recognize a good match?'
  • 想见诗题画壁前，慈云大士定相怜 - Means 'I imagine seeing poems inscribed on the wall before the compassionate cloud deity must surely pity us.'
  • 珍珠字比相思子，红豆丛生佛座边 - Means 'Pearl-like characters compared to the seeds of longing, red beans growing around the Buddha's seat.'
  • 不道吟情尔许浓，转来萧寺记芳踪 - Means 'It was not expected that the poetic sentiment would be so intense, and now at the temple, I remember her graceful presence.'
  • 十郎薄幸孙郎密，一样蓬山隔万重 - Means 'Ten Gentlemen are unfaithful, while Sun Gentleman is close; both are separated by countless mountains.'
  • 别时无语最凄惶，料得秋娘亦断肠 - Means 'When we parted, our silence was the most heartrending, and I imagine that Autumn Lady must also be heartbroken.'
  • 他日吴门重返棹，满腔愁绪在横塘 - Means 'One day, returning to Wu Gate on a boat, all my sorrow will be in the cross pond.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金阊 - Refers to Suzhou, also known as Jinling.
  • 少卿校书 - Refers to a female librarian or scholar named Shaoqing.
  • 欢生却扇 - A reference to a classical story about a romantic encounter.
  • 韵度绕梁 - A reference to a beautiful and lasting impression.
  • 吴下各姝 - Refers to the beauties of Wu (a region in China).
  • 申报 - Refers to the *Shen Bao* newspaper.
  • 清和 - Refers to a person named Qinghe.
  • 云英 - Refers to a person named Yunying.
  • 玉峰樵客 - Refers to a person named the Woodcutter of Yufeng Mountain.
  • 慈云大士 - Refers to a Buddhist deity.
  • 珍珠字比相思子 - Refers to a metaphorical comparison between pearl-like characters and the seeds of longing.
  • 红豆 - Refers to red beans, often associated with love and longing in Chinese literature.
  • 萧寺 - Refers to a temple.
  • 十郎 - Refers to a person named Ten Gentlemen.
  • 孙郎 - Refers to a person named Sun Gentleman.
  • 蓬山 - Refers to a mythical mountain.
  • 秋娘 - Refers to a person named Autumn Lady.
  • 吴门 - Refers to the entrance to Wu (a region in China).
  • 横塘 - Refers to a place name.

Proper Nouns

  • 少卿校书 - Refers to a female librarian or scholar named Shaoqing.
  • 清和 - Refers to a person named Qinghe.
  • 云英 - Refers to a person named Yunying.
  • 玉峰樵客 - Refers to a person named the Woodcutter of Yufeng Mountain.
  • 慈云大士 - Refers to a Buddhist deity.
  • 十郎 - Refers to a person named Ten Gentlemen.
  • 孙郎 - Refers to a person named Sun Gentleman.
  • 秋娘 - Refers to a person named Autumn Lady.
  • 吴门 - Refers to the entrance to Wu (a region in China).
  • 横塘 - Refers to a place name.

[625]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 和張少卿題虎阜寺壁絕句并序 - A poetic inscription in response to Zhang Shaoqing, inscribed on the wall of Hu Fu Temple, accompanied by a preface.
  • 風情月貌 - A poetic expression meaning 'graceful demeanor and beautiful appearance,' often used to describe someone's charm and beauty.
  • 蕙質蘭心 - A metaphor for a person with a gentle and refined nature, likened to the fragrant qualities of orchids and lilies.
  • 七字 - Refers to seven-character lines of poetry, a common form in Chinese classical poetry.
  • 傾倒一时 - Means to captivate or overwhelm people at the time.
  • 蘇小复生 - Refers to Su Xiaoxiao, a legendary courtesan from the Southern Song dynasty, used here as a literary allusion to a highly admired figure.
  • 玉峰樵客 - A literary pseudonym, likely referring to a poet or scholar.
  • 音节悲凉 - Describes a tone that is sorrowful and melancholic.
  • 名士倾城 - Refers to the admiration of a talented person, often used to describe a beloved figure.
  • 千载如一辙 - Means that something has remained the same over many centuries, emphasizing a consistent pattern.
  • 捐旧好 - Means to abandon old friends or relationships.
  • 博新欢 - Means to seek new pleasures or companionship.
  • 清和观察 - Likely refers to a government official, possibly a magistrate or governor.
  • 归里 - Means to return to one's hometown.
  • 芜香馆主 - A literary pseudonym, likely referring to a poet or scholar.
  • 花烛词 - Refers to poems written for wedding celebrations.
  • 红玉之归韩元帅 - Refers to a story about Hong Yu, a courtesan who married a military leader.
  • 盼盼之适张尚书 - Refers to a story about Pan Pan, a courtesan who married an official.
  • 志倾慕 - Means to express admiration or longing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张少卿 - A literary figure, likely a poet or scholar.
  • 虎阜寺 - A temple located in Suzhou, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 绝句 - A form of short poem, typically consisting of four lines.
  • 苏小 - A legendary courtesan from the Southern Song dynasty, often used as a symbol of beauty and talent.
  • 玉峰樵客 - A literary pseudonym, likely referring to a poet or scholar.
  • 清和观察 - A government official, possibly a magistrate or governor.
  • 芜香馆主 - A literary pseudonym, likely referring to a poet or scholar.
  • 红玉之归韩元帅 - A story about Hong Yu, a courtesan who married a military leader.
  • 盼盼之适张尚书 - A story about Pan Pan, a courtesan who married an official.

Proper Nouns

  • 张少卿 - A literary figure, likely a poet or scholar.
  • 虎阜寺 - A temple located in Suzhou.
  • 苏小 - A legendary courtesan from the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 玉峰樵客 - A literary pseudonym, likely referring to a poet or scholar.
  • 清和观察 - A government official, possibly a magistrate or governor.
  • 芜香馆主 - A literary pseudonym, likely referring to a poet or scholar.
  • 红玉 - A courtesan who married a military leader.
  • 韩元帅 - A military leader.
  • 盼盼 - A courtesan who married an official.
  • 张尚书 - An official.

[626]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 白首相偕 - Metaphor for an enduring relationship.
  • 梦醒樊川 - Refers to the poet Du Fu's place of exile, symbolizing hardship.
  • 薄幸 - Means unfaithful or unkind.
  • 眉峰 - Poetic way of referring to the eyebrows, often used to describe beauty or sorrow.
  • 和原韵 - Composing a poem in the same rhyme scheme as another.
  • 长门之赋 - Famous poem by Sima Xiangru, expressing the sorrow of a woman in the palace.
  • 娲皇无计补情天 - Metaphor for the impossibility of repairing broken emotions.
  • 剩得花枝伴月眠 - Describes loneliness using the metaphor of flowers and the moon.
  • 惆怅题诗分彩笔，当初一面竟无缘 - Expresses regret that they never had the chance to meet the person they admire.
  • 拟嫁张星路不前，新怀旧梦总相怜 - Describes mixed feelings of new emotions and old memories.
  • 那堪回首长堤柳，万绪情丝绾两边 - Expresses sorrow using the metaphor of willows and tangled threads.
  • 虎阜清流蠡水浓，画桡一去渺无踪 - Describes the sense of loss using the metaphor of a boat disappearing into the distance.
  • 不知此后门如海，望断蓬山又几重 - Expresses the sense of distance and unreachable dreams using the metaphor of the sea and distant mountains.
  • 画眉无意更凄惶，令我同回九曲肠 - Describes deep sorrow using the metaphor of drawing eyebrows and a twisted intestine.
  • 一样天涯悲冷落，潇潇暮雨听横塘 - Describes loneliness and sorrow using the metaphor of the ends of the earth and rain falling on a pond.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 观察者 - Refers to someone who observes or witnesses events.
  • 樊川 - A place associated with the poet Du Fu, often used to symbolize exile or hardship.
  • 薄幸 - A term used to describe someone who is unfaithful or unkind.
  • 长门之赋 - Famous poem by Sima Xiangru, expressing the sorrow of a woman in the palace.
  • 娲皇 - Nüwa, the goddess who mended the sky, often used in poetry to symbolize the repair of broken things.
  • 张星 - A metaphor for marriage, often used in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 虎阜 - A mountain, often used in poetry to describe a scenic location.
  • 画桡 - Refers to a boat, often used in poetry to describe travel or departure.
  • 蓬山 - A mythical mountain, often used in poetry to symbolize distant places or unreachable dreams.
  • 横塘 - A pond, often used in poetry to describe a place of reflection or sorrow.

Proper Nouns

  • 观察者 - A general term for someone who observes or witnesses events.
  • 樊川 - A place associated with the poet Du Fu, often used to symbolize exile or hardship.
  • 薄幸 - A term used to describe someone who is unfaithful or unkind.
  • 长门之赋 - Famous poem by Sima Xiangru, expressing the sorrow of a woman in the palace.
  • 娲皇 - Nüwa, the goddess who mended the sky, often used in poetry to symbolize the repair of broken things.
  • 张星 - A metaphor for marriage, often used in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 虎阜 - A mountain, often used in poetry to describe a scenic location.
  • 画桡 - Refers to a boat, often used in poetry to describe travel or departure.
  • 蓬山 - A mythical mountain, often used in poetry to symbolize distant places or unreachable dreams.
  • 横塘 - A pond, often used in poetry to describe a place of reflection or sorrow.

[627]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秀骨豐肌 - Describes a person with a slender yet plump figure, often used to depict beauty.
  • 眉目如畫 - A metaphor for eyes and eyebrows that are as beautiful as a painting.
  • 望之若神仙中人 - Means 'looks like someone from the realm of immortals,' emphasizing extraordinary beauty.
  • 引刀自刺其臂 - A dramatic action where the girl cuts her own arm with a knife, symbolizing her determination.
  • 血溢襟袖 - Describes blood flowing onto her clothes, indicating the severity of the injury.
  • 凝睇之 - Means 'to gaze fixedly,' showing deep attention.
  • 玉蕊琼英 - Metaphors for precious and beautiful things, here used to describe the girl's appearance.
  • 未足方喻 - Means 'cannot be adequately described,' emphasizing the girl's exceptional beauty.
  • 玉人如可購 - Means 'if the jade person could be bought,' referring to the girl as a precious object.
  • 何吝明珠一斛哉 - Means 'why would one hesitate to offer a measure of pearls,' expressing willingness to pay a high price.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邗江 - A place name in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 毗陵太守幕 - Refers to the office of the governor of Pizhong (modern-day Changzhou), indicating the social status of the traveler.
  • 維揚 - Another name for Yangzhou, emphasizing the setting.
  • 某紳 - Refers to a local gentleman or official who intervened on behalf of the girl.
  • 邑宰 - Refers to the magistrate of the county, indicating the authority involved in the intervention.
  • 越人張秋十 - Refers to a man from Yue (Zhejiang) named Zhang Qiushi, providing a specific identity for the traveler.
  • 郭翔步 - Means 'walking outside the city walls,' indicating a leisurely activity.
  • 野花蓬勃，好鳥勾 - Describes the natural scenery, emphasizing the beauty of the surroundings.
  • 掩映竹籬茅舍 - Means 'hidden behind bamboo fences and thatched cottages,' describing the rural setting.
  • 老身衣食，皆賴女十指 - Means 'my old age and food depend on her fingers,' emphasizing the mother's reliance on her daughter.
  • 嫁之則殘朽何以自存 - Means 'if she is married away, how will the old woman survive,' expressing concern for the mother's future.
  • 若郎君果欲得而甘心 - Means 'if the young master truly desires it,' indicating a conditional statement.
  • 非五百金不可 - Means 'it cannot be less than five hundred gold,' specifying the required amount.
  • 生檢點囊資，十不及一 - Means 'the young man checked his purse and found it lacked even a tenth,' indicating financial insufficiency.

Proper Nouns

  • 雙齡 - The name of the girl.
  • 邗江 - A place name in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 毗陵太守幕 - The office of the governor of Pizhong (modern-day Changzhou).
  • 維揚 - Another name for Yangzhou.
  • 某紳 - A local gentleman or official.
  • 邑宰 - The magistrate of the county.
  • 越人張秋十 - A man from Yue (Zhejiang) named Zhang Qiushi.
  • 郭翔步 - Walking outside the city walls.
  • 野花蓬勃，好鳥勾 - Natural scenery.
  • 掩映竹籬茅舍 - Hidden behind bamboo fences and thatched cottages.
  • 玉蕊琼英 - Precious and beautiful things.
  • 未足方喻 - Cannot be adequately described.
  • 玉人如可購 - If the jade person could be bought.
  • 何吝明珠一斛哉 - Why would one hesitate to offer a measure of pearls.
  • 遽請鄰媪 - Immediately asked the elderly neighbor.
  • 以意示其母 - Conveyed his intentions to her mother.
  • 老身衣食，皆賴女十指 - My old age and food depend on her fingers.
  • 嫁之則殘朽何以自存 - If she is married away, how will the old woman survive.
  • 若郎君果欲得而甘心 - If the young master truly desires it.
  • 非五百金不可 - It cannot be less than five hundred gold.
  • 生檢點囊資，十不及一 - The young man checked his purse and found it lacked even a tenth.

[628]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 然思无术，嗒焉若丧 - Describes a state of being without a method of thought, leading to a desolate feeling.
  • 谓母曰 - Introduces a direct speech, where the girl speaks to her mother.
  • 睹郎君气宇不凡，必非长于贫贱者 - Observes that the young man has an extraordinary presence and is certainly not from a poor background.
  • 儿若得所，母无忧矣 - If the child finds a suitable place, the mother will have no worries.
  • 何求重值耶 - Why seek a high price?
  • 母不忍拂 - The mother is unwilling to go against [the request].
  • 廉其价而遣之 - Bargains the price and lets her go.
  • 生遂献囊，载美而归 - The man then offers a bag and returns with a beautiful person.
  • 夫人贤且勤 - The lady is virtuous and diligent.
  • 见双龄妩媚可人 - Sees two young ones who are charming and pleasing.
  • 顿生怜爱 - Suddenly feels compassion and affection.
  • 典质钗钏 - Pawns jewelry.
  • 为女制衣履 - Makes clothes and shoes for the girl.
  • 令生抚么弦也 - Instructs the man to play the smallest string.
  • 女事夫人亦良谨 - The girl serves the lady very carefully and diligently.
  • 不苟言笑 - Does not speak or laugh frivolously.
  • 米盐琐屑 - Small daily necessities.
  • 能代夫人经纪 - Can manage household affairs on behalf of the lady.
  • 向道是画中人 - Used to believe she was a person from a painting.
  • 不能操井臼 - Cannot handle household chores.
  • 今若此，吾何虑焉 - Now like this, what do I have to worry about?
  • 田百亩，桑五十株 - One hundred acres of land and fifty mulberry trees.
  • 女缫丝织绢 - The girl spins silk and weaves silk.
  • 光洁无匹 - Smooth and shiny, unmatched.
  • 贾人踵门争购之 - Merchants visit and compete to buy it.
  • 得其值，纳太平之赋有余 - Gets its value and pays the peace tax with plenty left over.
  • 生由是名花相对，无志功名 - The man is with a beautiful woman and has no ambition for fame and glory.
  • 亦不复出，游吴会 - No longer goes out and travels to Wu and Kuaiji.
  • 尝有一绝句云 - Once there was a short poem that says.
  • 琴剑飘零久惜身 - Musical instruments and weapons, symbolizing a life of wandering, long have I cherished my body.
  • 温柔乡里作齐人 - In the land of softness and tenderness, to become a person of Qi.
  • 功名二字休提问 - Do not ask about fame and glory.
  • 常对名花悟夙因 - Often facing a beautiful woman, realizes past karma.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 名花 - Refers to a beautiful woman, often used metaphorically in classical Chinese literature.
  • 温柔乡 - A metaphor for a comfortable and pleasant environment, often associated with romantic or luxurious settings.
  • 齐人 - Refers to a person from the state of Qi, one of the warring states during the Spring and Autumn and Warring States periods.
  • 功名 - Refers to fame and glory, often associated with official positions and achievements in classical Chinese society.
  • 夙因 - Refers to past karma or former causes, a concept from Buddhism and Daoism.

[629]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 泺阳女子题壁诗 - Title meaning 'Poem on the Wall by a Girl from Lüyang.' '题壁' (tié bì) is an idiomatic expression meaning 'to inscribe on a wall.'
  • 白头之约 - Metaphorical expression meaning a lifelong commitment or promise.
  • 橐笔出游幕府 - '橐笔' (tuó bǐ) means to carry a pen (i.e., to write), and '出游幕府' (chū yóu mù fǔ) means to travel out as a guest in a military camp or government office. Together, it means to travel as a writer or scholar.
  • 意里连环绾不成 - Metaphorical expression meaning that the emotional ties or commitments are unbreakable, like a linked chain.
  • 琵琶有怨嗟鸳侣 - Metaphorical expression using the image of a pípá (a musical instrument) to express sorrow or lamentation for a couple (鸳侣).
  • 翡翠无温听雁更 - Metaphorical expression using the image of a fèicuì (a precious stone) to suggest a lack of warmth or comfort, and '听雁更' (tīng yàn gēng) refers to listening to the sound of geese, often used in poetry to evoke feelings of longing or loneliness.
  • 黄金何日赎残生 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'when will gold (wealth) redeem my remaining life?' It suggests a desperate plea for help or salvation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泺阳女子雪侬 - '泺阳' (luò yáng) refers to a place, and '雪侬' (xuě nóng) is likely a nickname for the girl, possibly meaning 'snowy' or 'pure.'
  • 西溪生 - The name of the man who made the promise with the girl.
  • 豫客 - Refers to a guest from Henan (豫), a province in China.
  • 中和店 - The name of the inn where the guest stayed.
  • 胡笳蔡女 - Refers to a historical or legendary figure, possibly a woman who played the huqin (a traditional Chinese stringed instrument).
  • 赵郡美人 - Refers to a beautiful woman from Zhaozhou (赵郡), a historical region in China.
  • 叱利 - A place name, possibly referring to a foreign land or a specific location in Chinese history.

Proper Nouns

  • 泺阳 - A place name.
  • 西溪生 - The name of the man.
  • 豫客 - A guest from Henan.
  • 中和店 - The name of the inn.
  • 胡笳蔡女 - A historical or legendary figure.
  • 赵郡 - A historical region.
  • 叱利 - A place name.

[630]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一度风流千贯钱 - Metaphorical expression meaning that a single moment of beauty or charm is worth thousands of strings of coins (a unit of currency). Emphasizes the value of beauty or charm.
  • 月老姻缘簿 - Refers to the 'Book of Marriages' kept by the deity Moon Matchmaker (月老), who is responsible for arranging marriages in Chinese folklore.
  • 一曰缘，二曰貌，三曰财 - Tripartite classification of the three factors that determine marriage: destiny (缘), appearance (貌), and wealth (财).
  • 有情眷属 - Refers to a loving couple or a harmonious marital relationship.
  • 缘尽则止 - Means that when the destined connection ends, the relationship ceases.
  • 白首曾盟 - Refers to a lifelong oath or promise made between spouses.
  • 绿窗已字 - Refers to a woman who has been married (绿窗 is a euphemism for a woman's home, and 已字 means 'already married').
  • 成陌路 - Means to become strangers or enemies.
  • 芙蓉出水 - Metaphor for a woman's beauty, comparing her to a lotus flower emerging from the water.
  • 杨柳临风 - Metaphor for a woman's grace, comparing her to a willow tree swaying in the wind.
  • 阿环之苗裔 - Refers to a descendant of Lady Aohan, a famous beauty from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 芳龄十八 - Polite way of saying 'eighteen years old.'
  • 待字闺中 - Refers to a young woman waiting to be married.
  • 东李西张，肥钱瘦赵 - Humorous way of saying that she has had many suitors, but none have been successful.
  • 得婿良难 - Means that finding a suitable husband is difficult.
  • 迄无成说 - Means that there has been no resolution or agreement.
  • 天宝社演剧 - Refers to a theatrical performance by the 'Tianbao Society,' likely a local drama troupe.
  • 菊部大佳 - Refers to the actors or performers being excellent.
  • 观剧者如火如荼 - Means that the audience was enthusiastic and numerous.
  • 万人空巷 - Means that so many people came to watch that the streets were empty.
  • 斗新妆 - Refers to people competing in their new and elaborate makeup.
  • 椿萱并茂 - Refers to a mother and father who are both healthy and thriving.
  • 弟已成童 - Means that her younger brother has grown up.
  • 日罄室往 - Means that she visits the theater every day, even going to the point of emptying her room.
  • 门加钥焉 - Means that the door is locked to prevent her from going out.
  • 纨绔少年 - Refers to a young, wealthy, and possibly idle man.
  • 惊鸿游龙 - Metaphor for someone who is exceptionally beautiful or graceful.
  • 眼溜眉波 - Refers to the young man's gaze and expression, suggesting his admiration or attraction.
  • 色授神与 - Means that he is entranced by her appearance and is giving her his attention.
  • 就女旁木立 - Means that he stands motionless beside her, likely in awe or admiration.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 月老 - The Chinese deity responsible for arranging marriages, often depicted holding a book of marriages (姻缘簿).
  • 阿环 - A famous beauty from the Tang Dynasty, known for her exceptional beauty and talent.
  • 天宝社 - Likely a reference to a local drama troupe or theater group, possibly named after the 'Tianbao' era of the Tang Dynasty.
